objects.name: CVE-2025-13915
objects.description: A critical authentication bypass flaw in IBM API Connect allows remote attackers to gain unauthorized access without valid credentials due to an implementation weakness. The vulnerability impacts IBM API Connect versions V10.0.8.0 through V10.0.8.5 and V10.0.11.0. It is classified under CWE-305 (Authentication Bypass by Primary Weakness) and CWE-287 (Improper Authentication). The flaw carries a CVSS v3.1 base score of 9.8 (CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H), indicating remote exploitability, no user interaction required, and complete compromise of confidentiality, integrity, and availability. No evidence of active exploitation or public exploit circulation has been reported. IBM has released interim fixes and recommends immediate patching.

objects.name: IBM API Connect
objects.description: IBM API Connect is a widely used enterprise platform for managing application programming interfaces (APIs). Versions V10.0.8.0 through V10.0.8.5 and V10.0.11.0 are affected by CVE-2025-13915, a critical authentication bypass vulnerability.
